elements.text:
A series of short papers by German, French and American workers followed Shevyrev's publications. 

C. Rumbold; The Injection of Chemicals into Chestnut Trees, American Journal of Botany, Vol. 7, No. 1 (Jan., 1920), pp. 1-20 
Chestnut- tree blight (Endothia parasitica (Murr. )
Injection of > 40 substances into the trunks of more than 150 orchard-grown chestnut trees: simple salts, various chromates, salts of silver and mercury, phenols, citric acid, salicylic acid, and infusions made from healthy and diseased chestnut bark.
None of the injected trees recovered during the five years of her investigations (1915 to 1920) and the work was abandoned "without conclusive results".
Tree Injection in the first half of XX century
